comment_text.sn33.6-10:1.6: From here the discourses are only differentiated by the verb used for understanding.

root_text.sn33.6-10:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn33.6-10:1.3: “ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo, yānimāni anekavihitāni diṭṭhigatāni loke uppajjanti—
root_text.sn33.6-10:1.4: sassato lokoti vā, asassato lokoti vā …pe…
root_text.sn33.6-10:1.5: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā”ti?
root_text.sn33.6-10:1.11: viññāṇe kho, vaccha, adassanā …pe… viññāṇanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya adassanā …pe….

translation_text.sn33.6-10:0.3: Five Discourses on Not Seeing Form, Etc.
translation_text.sn33.6-10:1.2: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn33.6-10:1.3: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why these various misconceptions arise in the world? …”
translation_text.sn33.6-10:1.6: “Vaccha, it is because of not seeing form …”
translation_text.sn33.6-10:1.11: “… consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation …”
